title: System tray design: minimize-to-tray with fallback, embedded badged icons, per-session notification routing
decision: Sprint 19 ships a system tray that aggregates needs-attention counts across daemons. Three coupled choices: (1) closing the window minimizes to tray rather than quitting, with a safety fallback to quit-on-close when no tray host exists (createTray returns null); explicit quit via tray menu or Ctrl+Q. (2) The tray icon is embedded as base64 PNG data URLs in a generated module (src/main/tray-icons.ts) with pre-generated numeric badge variants (base, 1-9, 9+), because packaged builds bundle only dist/** and tsc does not copy assets, and Linux trays have no native numeric badge. (3) Notification priority is per-session (high/normal/silent), stored in preferences keyed by composite session id, consulted at the needs-attention transition; silent suppresses only the OS notification, never status/tray accounting.

context.task_description: Sprint 19 (System Tray & Notification Routing), Flow II milestone.
assumptions: Linux is the primary platform; tray support varies by desktop environment
Packaged app files include only dist/**, so runtime assets must be embedded or staged
Users want to mute noisy sessions and escalate important ones

options.label: Minimize-to-tray with fallback + embedded badged icons
options.description: Hide-to-tray default, fall back to quit-on-close if no tray host. Embed base64 icon variants (with numeric badges) generated from build/icon.png. Per-session priority in prefs.

options.label: Tray additive only (no minimize-to-tray)
options.description: Keep quit-on-close; tray only shows a badge while the window is open. Rejected: the user chose minimize-to-tray with a guardrail.

options.label: Loose icon asset file + numeric overlay at runtime
options.description: Ship a PNG file and composite the count at runtime. Rejected: packaged builds don't include loose assets, and runtime image compositing needs an image lib unavailable in main.

rationale: The user explicitly chose minimize-to-tray with a safety fallback after the Linux-tray-reliability risk was surfaced. Embedding pre-generated badged icons is the only robust way to show a numeric count on Linux given the packaging constraints and absence of a runtime image library. Per-session priority in prefs reuses the existing persistence + broadcast path and keeps the daemon protocol untouched.

risk.rollback: Revert Sprint 19 commits; tray.ts/tray-icons.ts are additive and main.ts changes are localized.

outcome.notes: Tray dnd-independent; numeric-on-icon satisfied via pre-generated variants. AppImage tray icon path inherits the same embedded approach.
